results.rationale: 'match' is (re)allocated at line 5029 (concat_pattern_with_buffer_match). On the path where it is not NULL and not a duplicate, it reaches line 5050-5053. If ga_grow(&ga,1) fails at 5052, the code does 'goto cleanup' without freeing 'match'. The cleanup label only calls ga_clear_strings(&ga), which frees entries already stored in the growable array but not the current 'match' that has not yet been appended, so the 5029 allocation leaks on the OOM/ga_grow-failure path. This matches a PartialLeak (leak on some feasible paths while other paths transfer ownership to ga or free it).
results.fix_suggestion: Free 'match' before 'goto cleanup' on the ga_grow failure path, e.g. add 'vim_free(match);' before line 5053.

results.fix_suggestion: Call clear_diffin(&dio->dio_new) before the continue at line 1066 (diff_file failure), or move dio_new cleanup so it runs on every loop iteration regardless of early continue.

results.rationale: dict_alloc() at line 5978 allocates a dict_T. If list_append_dict(l, d) fails at line 5980, the function returns at 5981 without freeing d (no dict_free/dict_unref), leaking the just-allocated dict. The success path transfers ownership to the list, but the FAIL branch does not, so this is a feasible leak path.
results.fix_suggestion: On the list_append_dict failure branch, free the dict before returning, e.g. call dict_unref(d) (or dict_free(d)) prior to the return at line 5981.

results.rationale: dict_alloc() at line 5760 returns a dict with refcount 0. If list_append_dict fails at 5762, the function returns at 5763 without freeing d. list_append_dict only takes ownership (increments refcount and links the item) on success; on FAIL the dict is never appended, so the early return leaks the freshly allocated dict. Compare with line 5731 where the analogous list_alloc is freed on append failure.
results.fix_suggestion: On list_append_dict failure, free the dict before returning, e.g. `if (list_append_dict(l, d) == FAIL) { dict_unref(d); return; }`

results.rationale: dict_alloc() at line 9375 returns a dict with refcount 0; ownership is intended to transfer to mlist via list_append_dict (which increments the refcount and lets the caller free it). On the normal success path this is correct, and the later 'return FAIL' branches (9381-9411) are safe because mlist already owns d. However, line 9378 has a feasible leak: if list_append_dict fails (e.g. listitem_alloc OOM), d was allocated but never appended and never freed, so 'return FAIL' at 9379 leaks the dict. That single error path satisfies the never-free condition.
results.fix_suggestion: On the list_append_dict failure branch, free d before returning: 'if (list_append_dict(mlist, d) == FAIL) { dict_free(d); return FAIL; }' (or use dict_unref since refcount is still 0).

results.rationale: new_tuple is allocated at line 1360 but its refcount is only set (line 1379) and it is only attached to ltv (line 1378) for cleanup via clear_tv (line 1385) after the copy loop completes. If tuple_append_tv fails inside the loop, line 1372 returns FAIL while new_tuple is still allocated and not yet owned by ltv, so it is never freed. This is a feasible leak path. (Note: the list branch at 1342-1356 attaches l to ltv right away and has no failing append, so it does not have the same flaw.)
results.fix_suggestion: On the tuple_append_tv failure path, free new_tuple (e.g. tuple_unref/clear) before returning FAIL, or attach it to ltv and use a goto-cleanup pattern so clear_tv handles it.

results.rationale: highlight_get_attr_dict() returns a freshly allocated dict_T whose refcount is only incremented when dict_add_dict() succeeds. If dict_add_dict() returns FAIL the code goes to the 'error' label without releasing attr_dict, leaking it. Additionally the error handler uses vim_free(dict) rather than dict_unref(dict), so any attr_dict already owned by 'dict' (added at 5254/5267/5290) is not recursively freed when a later add fails (e.g. 5273/5276). Both give a feasible leak path for the allocation at 5265.
results.fix_suggestion: At the error label use dict_unref(dict) instead of vim_free(dict) so nested dicts are released, and call dict_unref(attr_dict) when dict_add_dict() returns FAIL before goto error.

results.rationale: part is freshly allocated at line 6527 and is only freed on the success path (vim_free(part) at 6547 before return OK). Every error branch inside this block uses 'goto fail' (6529, 6533, 6541, 6544). The reported path reaches 'goto fail' after part is already allocated (e.g. put_eol FAIL at 6532, fprintf at 6540, put_escstr/put_eol at 6543). Saber classifies this as a PartialLeak, meaning part is released on some paths but not on the fail path, which is consistent with a shared fail: label that frees buf but not the locally-allocated part. The slice does not include the fail: label body, so the leak cannot be 100% confirmed, but the asymmetry (freed only on the OK path) strongly indicates the error path leaks part.
results.fix_suggestion: Initialize part = NULL at declaration and add vim_free(part) at the fail: label (alongside vim_free(buf)), so the error paths release part instead of only the success path.

results.rationale: Line 1595 allocates `line` via vim_strsave (owned heap memory). It is then handed to ga_add_string at 1598. In vim, ga_add_string internally makes its own copy (vim_strsave) of the input and stores that copy in the grow array, so it does not take ownership of `line`. Consequently `line` is never freed: on the ga_add_string==FAIL branch (1598 True -> goto errret) the just-allocated `line` was not stored in sp->buflines, so errret's array cleanup (ga_clear_strings) cannot reach it, and on the success branch `line` is likewise never freed. This matches Saber's PartialLeak: the conditional cleanup at errret frees the array contents but leaves the original `line` allocation leaked.
results.fix_suggestion: Avoid the redundant ownership-less copy: pass ml_get(curr_lnum) directly to ga_add_string (which copies internally), or explicitly vim_free(line) after the ga_add_string call on both success and FAIL paths.

results.rationale: At line 1595 `line` is freshly allocated via vim_strsave. On success it is handed to ga_add_string, which in Vim stores the pointer directly (ownership transfer, no copy), so the success path is fine. But on the FAIL branch at line 1598, ga_add_string did not store `line` (ga_grow failed), yet the code jumps to errret at 1599 without freeing `line`. The errret cleanup clears the buflines array but not this just-allocated local, leaking it on the FAIL path. This matches the reported conditional path (1598 True).
results.fix_suggestion: Add `vim_free(line);` before the `goto errret;` on the ga_add_string FAIL branch (line 1599).

results.rationale: On the success path, list_append_dict appends the dict to retlist and increments its refcount, so ownership transfers to the list and it is freed with the list (not a leak). However, on the error path at line 1831-1832, if list_append_dict returns FAIL (e.g., listitem_alloc OOM), the dict allocated at 1827 is neither appended nor freed before the function returns, leaking the allocation. This is a genuine, though OOM-only, memory leak.
results.fix_suggestion: Before returning on the list_append_dict FAIL branch, release the dict, e.g. call dict_unref(dict) (or free it) at line 1831-1832 prior to return.

results.rationale: str_foldcase(name, namelen, name_folded, MAXKEYWLEN+1) returns either a pointer into the stack buffer name_folded or a freshly heap-allocated buffer when the case-folded result does not fit the provided buffer. The returned pointer name_ic is only consumed via STRCPY into kp->keyword (line 4426) and is never freed before the function returns. On the path where str_foldcase allocates (folded text exceeds MAXKEYWLEN+1, which can happen with multibyte/UTF-8 case folding that changes length), the allocation leaks. No free occurs on any exit path.
results.fix_suggestion: After copying name_ic into kp->keyword, free it if it was heap-allocated: if (name_ic != name_folded && name_ic != name) vim_free(name_ic);

results.rationale: saved_name is allocated by vim_strsave at 6661. On the non-def path, if ga_grow fails (6677) the code does 'goto theend' without freeing saved_name, leaking it; ownership is only transferred to dr->dr_name at 6681 after the grow succeeds. The theend block only clears argvars, never saved_name. The in_def_function branch may also leak saved_name if add_defer_function does not return OK. This is a feasible never-free path.
results.fix_suggestion: In the theend block (or before the ga_grow failure goto), vim_free(saved_name) when ownership was not transferred to dr->dr_name; track transfer with a flag and free saved_name on all early-exit paths.

results.rationale: At line 2358 'line' is allocated via concat_str and at 2362 'tofree = line', so both point to heap memory that the normal path frees at a 'theend' label. Inside the `=expr handling block entered at 2406, the error exits at line 2429 (compile_expr0 FAIL) and line 2436 (missing backtick) use 'return NULL' directly, bypassing the cleanup and leaking the buffer held by line/tofree. The reported path through 2428/2433 confirms these early returns are reachable.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the 'return NULL' at lines 2429 and 2436 with 'goto theend' (or free tofree/line before returning) so the allocated buffer is released on these error paths.

results.rationale: At line 2358 concat_str allocates a new buffer assigned to line, and at 2361-2362 the buffer is handed to tofree (tofree = line). The normal exit frees tofree via the 'theend' label. However, the has_expr block entered at 2406 contains early 'return NULL' statements at 2429 (when compile_expr0 fails) and 2436 (missing backtick) that bypass the theend cleanup, so the buffer held by tofree/line is never released on these failure paths. The path is feasible: tofree must be non-NULL (may_get_cmd_block set it) and has_expr with a '`=' substring leads into the block where compile_expr0 can fail.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the bare 'return NULL' statements inside the has_expr block (lines 2429 and 2436) with 'goto theend' (or vim_free(tofree) before returning) so the allocated buffer is released on these failure paths.

results.rationale: At line 3365 the alloc result is assigned directly into tv1->vval.v_string, which previously held the old string saved in local s1 (line 3361). If alloc returns NULL (line 3366), tv1->vval.v_string is now NULL and the error path calls clear_ppconst and returns. clear_ppconst frees the ppconst entries, but tv1's string pointer has been overwritten to NULL, so the original buffer pointed to by s1 is never freed and leaks. (s2 is still reachable via the ppconst entry, so only s1 leaks - a partial leak, matching the PartialLeak report.)
results.fix_suggestion: Allocate into a temporary pointer and only assign to tv1->vval.v_string on success; on NULL, free s1 (and clear remaining ppconst) before returning FAIL.

results.rationale: At line 1970, when dict_add succeeds, ownership of the item allocated at 1965 transfers to dict d, and on dict_add failure dictitem_free is called (so the item itself is handled). However, after the item is added to d, several error paths in the loop use 'return FAIL' (lines 1981, 1987, 2018, 2024) instead of 'goto failret', bypassing the dict_unref(d) cleanup at 2046. On a feasible path (dict_add succeeds, then e.g. missing colon at 1975 -> return FAIL at 1981), dict d and all items added to it, including the item from 1965, leak. The tool's PartialLeak is justified: the item is freed only on the dict_add-failure branch and is otherwise tied to d, which is not always released.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the bare 'return FAIL' statements inside the loop (lines 1981, 1987, 2018, 2024) with 'goto failret' so dict_unref(d) always runs and the dict plus its items are released on every error path.

results.rationale: At line 3365 the allocation result is written directly into tv1->vval.v_string, overwriting the saved original string s1. If alloc() returns NULL (a feasible path, confirmed by the NULL check at 3366), clear_ppconst() at 3368 frees the typvals, but tv1->vval.v_string is now NULL, so the original s1 buffer is never freed and is leaked. s2 is still freed via tv2, making this a partial leak of s1 on the allocation-failure path.
results.fix_suggestion: Allocate into a temporary pointer (e.g. char_u *res = alloc(...)) and only assign tv1->vval.v_string on success; on NULL, free s1 (and ensure s2 is handled) before clear_ppconst/return to avoid leaking s1.

results.rationale: type_name() allocates into ret_free at line 2730. On the normal path it is freed at 2738, but if ga_grow fails at 2734 the code jumps to the failed label (2743) which only frees arg_free and clears ga, never freeing ret_free. So on the allocation-failure path ret_free leaks.
results.fix_suggestion: Free ret_free before goto failed at line 2735 (or add vim_free(ret_free) in the failed cleanup path).

results.rationale: ALLOC_ONE(type_T) at line 149 can return NULL on allocation failure, but line 150 immediately dereferences ret with `*ret = *type` without any NULL check. This is a feasible null-pointer dereference. Note the allocated memory itself is returned to the caller (line 167), so there is no leak in this function; the genuine defect is the missing NULL guard.
results.fix_suggestion: Add a NULL check after the allocation: `ret = ALLOC_ONE(type_T); if (ret == NULL) return NULL;` before `*ret = *type;`.

results.fix_suggestion: Call vim_free(ret_free) before goto failed at line 2735, or add vim_free(ret_free) in the failed: cleanup block.

results.rationale: buf is allocated at line 1057 for a continuation string. It is only freed on the truncated/garbled path (vim_free at 1069) or has its ownership transferred to value->bv_string/bv_tofree on the quoted-string branch (1147/1158, with bv_allocated=TRUE since allocated is set at 1040). However, after the for-loop at 1061 completes normally, p=buf points to the accumulated content, and if that content is not a quoted string the code falls into the digit branch (1105, BVAL_NR), the empty branch (1170), or the else/break (1175). On all of those branches buf is neither stored nor freed, and the function continues or returns TRUE at 1178, leaking buf. Since viminfo input is file-derived/untrusted, a corrupted continuation whose content begins with a digit or comma reaches the leaking path, matching Saber's PartialLeak (freed on some paths, leaked on others).
results.fix_suggestion: After the for-loop, free buf on the non-string branches (BVAL_NR at 1105, BVAL_EMPTY at 1170, and the else/break at 1175) when buf != NULL and ownership was not transferred to value->bv_string, e.g. add vim_free(buf) before those branches finish or before the function returns.

results.rationale: buf allocated at 1057 is freed only in the truncation/garble error branch (1069). On normal loop completion, ownership of buf is transferred only when the parsed value is a string (1111-1162); in the digit (1105), empty (1170), and else-break (1175) branches buf is neither stored nor freed before the function returns at 1178, leaking it for malformed continuation input.
results.fix_suggestion: Track buf ownership and vim_free(buf) before returning whenever it is not handed off to value->bv_string/bv_tofree (e.g., free buf at function exit if not transferred).
